name: Tacoma Longshoremen
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving ILWU longshoremen and families in the Tacoma, WA area since 1963, offering savings accounts, loans, and financial services.
description_long: Tacoma Longshoremen Credit Union is a not-for-profit, state-chartered financial cooperative established in February 1963 to serve local longshore workers in Tacoma, Washington. The credit union was founded on the principle that workers could save together and access low-cost loans from a member-owned institution rather than relying on traditional banks. Today, it operates as a financial cooperative with a specific membership base tied to the International Longshore and Warehouse Union (ILWU) locals in the Pacific Northwest. The credit union provides core banking services including savings accounts, checking accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and personal loans. Members can access online banking with enhanced security features, including security image verification to prevent phishing attacks. The institution emphasizes member education through programs like Financial Literacy Week, demonstrating a commitment to financial wellness beyond transactional services. What distinguishes Tacoma Longshoremen Credit Union is its specialized focus and restricted membership model. Unlike banks open to the general public, membership is limited to ILWU members from Local #23, #22, and #47, casual longshore workers with 2,000+ hours on the ILWU Local #23 list, and families of existing members. This creates a tight-knit community of workers in the same industry, fostering the "people helping people" philosophy core to credit union operations. The institution's 60+ year history demonstrates stability and deep roots in the longshoreman community. Tacoma Longshoremen Credit Union is best suited for ILWU members and their families seeking a community-focused, member-owned alternative to traditional banking. Those interested in competitive rates from a not-for-profit institution with personal service will benefit most. However, potential members should note that membership eligibility is restrictive and not available to the general public, making it inaccessible to those outside the ILWU network.
services: Online banking with enhanced security features and phishing protection
Membership for ILWU union members and affiliates
pros: Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means profits are returned to members rather than shareholders
Specialized focus on longshore workers creates industry-specific understanding and community trust
Enhanced online security features including security image and passphrase verification to prevent phishing
Established since 1963 with 60+ years of stability and experience serving the longshoreman community
Offers financial education programming including Financial Literacy Week events for member families
Serves multi-generational families through spousal, children, and grandchildren membership eligibility
Personalized, considerate service approach stated as operating principle
cons: Restricted membership eligibility limits access to only ILWU members and their relatives, excluding general public
Limited geographic reach serves primarily Tacoma and Olympia, WA areas only
Website content does not detail specific loan products, interest rates, fees, or account minimums for comparison
No information provided about product variety, maximum loan amounts, or competitive rate structures
Small institution may have fewer branch locations and services compared to larger regional or national credit unions
best_for: ILWU Local #23 and #22 members in Tacoma seeking member-owned banking with community focus
Longshoreman families wanting to preserve savings and access loans within their professional community
Casual longshore workers on the ILWU Local #23 ID casual list with 2,000+ hours seeking cooperative banking
Spouses and children of credit union members looking for family-inclusive financial services
diagnosis: Tacoma Longshoremen Credit Union is ideal for ILWU longshoremen and their families seeking a member-owned, community-focused financial institution with 60+ years of stability and industry-specific expertise. The main caveat is that membership is restricted to ILWU union members and their relatives—it is not available to the general public—and the institution operates only in the Tacoma and Olympia, WA areas.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
